examples.title: Accessing Global and Local Variables
examples.description: Demonstrates using :: to access global variable when local variable has same name
examples.explanation: The leading :: before x explicitly accesses the global variable, ignoring the local variable with the same name.

examples.title: Member Function Outside Class
examples.description: Shows how to define a member function outside the class using scope resolution
examples.explanation: The :: operator associates the outside function definition with the MyClass scope, making it a member of the class.

examples.title: Enum Class with Scope Resolution
examples.description: Demonstrates scoped enumerations requiring :: to access values
examples.explanation: Enum class constants are scoped to the enum type and require :: for access. This prevents naming conflicts.

examples.title: Base Class Member Access in Inheritance
examples.description: Shows using :: to access base class members from derived class
examples.explanation: :: allows explicit access to base class members even when they are overridden in the derived class.

examples.title: Static Member Definition
examples.description: Demonstrates defining static members outside class using ::
examples.explanation: Static member variables must be defined once globally using ClassName::memberName syntax.

examples.title: Namespace Usage with std::
examples.description: Shows proper usage of :: with namespaces
examples.explanation: Using std:: explicitly is preferred over 'using namespace std' to avoid potential naming conflicts.
